reasoning: The series CCLACBW027SBOG (seasonally adjusted weekly credit card loans and other revolving plans) generally exhibits a strong positive secular trend due to nominal spending growth and inflation. In non-recessionary periods, a 3-month period (from late October to late January) is more than sufficient for the underlying growth trend (historically ~5-8% annualized) to overcome weekly zero-mean noise. While non-seasonally adjusted credit card debt falls in January, the seasonal adjustment process mostly regularizes this. Assuming no sudden deep recession materialized between Oct 2025 and Jan 2026 that leads to sharp deleveraging, the value is highly likely to increase. Accounting for small risks of economic downturns or SA formula artifacts, I estimate an 87% chance of an increase.
